coopOptions: No Co-op / alternate pathways depending on catalog year
keyWarnings: Computer Science has a tight foundational sequence; delaying intro programming or data structures creates downstream risk.
Upper-level CS and SE courses depend on core systems / theory classes being finished in order.
Co-op timing matters because course sequencing and capstone planning can shift around work cycles.
Students should build technical portfolio / internship readiness early, not only rely on coursework.

mathAndScienceSignals: Science requirements support systems / computing depth
coreCourses.upperLevelRequirement: 6 additional 300- or 400-level CS or SE courses not already used elsewhere

samplePlanSignals: Intro programming must happen early; CS 171 and CS 172 are key sequence anchors.
CS 260 (Data Structures) is a major bottleneck because many upper-level courses build on it.
CS 270, CS 277, CS 281, and CS 283 define the theory/systems core and should not be pushed too late.
Capstone and upper-level specialization planning should begin before senior year.
mvpRecommendationRules: Flag if a student has not completed CS 171/172 or equivalent by the end of the first major year.
Treat CS 260 as a hard bottleneck and prioritize it as soon as prerequisites allow.
Recommend internships, co-op readiness, GitHub portfolio work, and project-based opportunities starting early.
If a student wants AI/ML, suggest earlier statistics/math and upper-level AI-aligned electives once core sequence is completed.
